 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-7m9s","title":"Integration Tests: Error Monitoring Hooks","description":"## Origin
Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-kl6p. Keep both repos in sync.

## Task
Create integration tests for error monitoring hooks (Sentry configuration).

## Why This Matters
- Ensures errors are captured consistently
- Prevents silent failures in production

## Test Scenarios
1. Simulated server error sends Sentry event (stubbed DSN)
2. Client error boundary captures and reports
3. RequestId included in Sentry context

## Logging Requirements
- Log capture calls with requestId
- Ensure no PII in payloads

## Files to Create
- tests/integration/monitoring/sentry-server.test.ts
- tests/integration/monitoring/sentry-client.test.ts

## Success Criteria
- Monitoring hooks wired correctly
- Events carry correlation IDs
- Tests run without external network calls

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-7sdv","title":"Reduce JS bundle size from 660KB to 300KB","description":"## Background
Bundle monitoring is now set up (size-limit, @next/bundle-analyzer).

Current state:
- Total JS: 660.58 KB gzipped
- Total CSS: 41.13 KB gzipped
- Target: Reduce JS to <300 KB

## Large Chunks Identified
- 336aa4397c308dd5.js - 431 KB (uncompressed)
- caebb29fdf4ff5c0.js - 268 KB
- 58cc495ad700f84c.js - 219 KB
- a8b97a5fd820d1df.js - 150 KB

## Optimization Strategies
1. Run ANALYZE=true bun run build to identify what's in each chunk
2. Dynamic imports for heavy components (syntax-highlighter, framer-motion)
3. Tree-shake unused icon imports (lucide-react)
4. Code-split routes with dynamic()
5. Lazy load below-fold components
6. Review large dependencies for alternatives

## Commands
- bun run analyze - Opens bundle analyzer
- bun run size-limit - Check current sizes
- bun run size-limit:check - JSON output for CI","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T18:13:10.543333705-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T18:13:24.656061981-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-7sdv","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-qg15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T18:13:34.803870085-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-7sdv","title":"Reduce JS bundle size from 660KB to 300KB","description":"## Background
Bundle monitoring is now set up (size-limit, @next/bundle-analyzer).

Current state:
- Total JS: 660.58 KB gzipped
- Total CSS: 41.13 KB gzipped
- Target: Reduce JS to <300 KB

## Large Chunks Identified
- 336aa4397c308dd5.js - 431 KB (uncompressed)
- caebb29fdf4ff5c0.js - 268 KB
- 58cc495ad700f84c.js - 219 KB
- a8b97a5fd820d1df.js - 150 KB

## Optimization Strategies
1. Run ANALYZE=true bun run build to identify what's in each chunk
2. Dynamic imports for heavy components (syntax-highlighter, framer-motion)
3. Tree-shake unused icon imports (lucide-react)
4. Code-split routes with dynamic()
5. Lazy load below-fold components
6. Review large dependencies for alternatives

## Commands
- bun run analyze - Opens bundle analyzer
- bun run size-limit - Check current sizes
- bun run size-limit:check - JSON output for CI","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T18:13:10.543333705-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T18:29:22.632754563-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-7sdv","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-qg15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T18:13:34.803870085-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-7so","title":"Build jfp CLI tool with robot-mode design -- Background: The CLI is agent-first, human-compatible.
